name: Model2Vec-RS
tagline: Fast static embedding inference in Rust for Model2Vec models
metaDescription: Model2Vec-RS is a Rust crate and CLI for low-latency inference with Model2Vec static embedding models. Load from Hugging Face or local paths and encode text fast.
overview: Model2Vec-RS is the official Rust implementation for running Model2Vec static embedding models. Model2Vec is a technique that turns sentence transformers into compact static embedding models, cutting model size and speeding up inference. This crate handles the inference side, so you can generate embeddings from text with low latency.

It is aimed at Rust developers who need embeddings inside an application or pipeline without the overhead of a full transformer runtime. You can use it as a library in your own code or as a standalone command-line tool for quick terminal-based encoding.

As an embedding inference tool, it loads pre-trained Model2Vec models directly from the Hugging Face Hub or from a local path, supports f32, f16, and i8 weights stored in safetensors files, and offers feature flags for native, local-only, and WebAssembly builds.
features: Loads Model2Vec models from the Hugging Face Hub by model ID or from a local path via from_pretrained
Alternative loaders for embedded and WASM use: from_bytes, from_owned, and from_borrowed for in-memory data
Supports f32, f16, and i8 weight types stored in safetensors files
Batch encoding with configurable max sequence length and batch size
Ships both a Rust library and a standalone CLI (encode-single, encode)
Feature flags for native, fancy-regex, local-only, and wasm runtime configurations

gettingStarted.intro: You can use Model2Vec-RS as a Rust library or as a standalone CLI tool. Both load models from the Hugging Face Hub or a local path.

steps.heading: Add the crate as a dependency
steps.body: Add model2vec-rs to your Rust project with Cargo.

steps.heading: Load a model and generate embeddings
steps.body: Load a model from the Hugging Face Hub (or a local path) and encode a list of sentences. The result is a Vec<Vec<f32>>.

steps.heading: Or install and use the CLI
steps.body: Install the executable in release mode, then encode a single sentence. Output is printed as JSON.
steps.code:
cargo install model2vec-rs
model2vec-rs encode-single "Hello world" "minishlab/potion-base-8M"

useCases: Generating text embeddings inside a Rust application or service with low latency
Quick terminal-based encoding of sentences or files via the CLI
Running static embeddings in embedded or WebAssembly environments using in-memory model loading
Batch-encoding documents for semantic search or retrieval indexing
